name: pganalyze PostgreSQL Performance Monitoring API
what_it_does: PostgreSQL performance monitoring platform with REST API — enabling database teams to continuously analyze query performance, index utilization, VACUUM/bloat health, and connection pool efficiency across PostgreSQL instances (self-hosted, RDS, Aurora, Cloud SQL, Azure Database), with AI-powered query insights and EXPLAIN plan analysis.
use_cases: Monitoring query performance via pganalyze API for slow query detection and pg_stat_statements analysis
Analyzing EXPLAIN plans via pganalyze for automatic query optimization recommendations
Tracking index usage via pganalyze API for unused index identification and missing index suggestions
Monitoring VACUUM health via pganalyze for table bloat and autovacuum efficiency tracking
Setting up alerting via pganalyze API for query regression and connection pool exhaustion detection
not_for: Application performance monitoring (use Datadog or New Relic for full APM)
Real-time query tracing at row level (pganalyze uses pg_stat_statements aggregates)
best_when: A PostgreSQL DBA or data engineering team wants deep query performance insights and automated optimization recommendations for Postgres instances across cloud and self-hosted environments.
avoid_when: You need monitoring for MySQL, MongoDB, or other non-Postgres databases, or need full application-level distributed tracing.

auth.notes: API key via Authorization: Token {api_key} header (Token not Bearer). API key from pganalyze account settings. REST API at app.pganalyze.com/api/v1/. Organization ID and server ID required for server-specific endpoints. pganalyze Collector: local agent installed on each PostgreSQL server that ships stats to pganalyze cloud. Self-hosted pganalyze Enterprise: on-premises deployment option. Webhook events: alert notifications on query regressions, vacuum issues, connection pool problems.

pricing.notes: US company (San Francisco). Founded 2012 by Lukas Fittl. Bootstrap/profitable company. PostgreSQL-only: deep Postgres expertise — pg_stat_statements, pg_stat_user_tables, VACUUM stats, EXPLAIN plans. Collector agent: lightweight Go binary installed on each DB server or in Kubernetes sidecar. AI insights: automated EXPLAIN plan analysis with index recommendations. VACUUM monitoring: table bloat, autovacuum worker tracking, transaction ID wraparound prevention. Connection pooling: integration with PgBouncer metrics. AWS RDS/Aurora: native integration via Performance Insights. Enterprise: self-hosted pganalyze for air-gapped or compliance requirements.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Token auth (not Bearer). Organization ID + Server ID required. Collector agent required on DB servers. PostgreSQL-only. US company. SOC2/GDPR. Per-server pricing. Self-hosted Enterprise option.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: API reads are fully idempotent. Alert configuration: idempotent by alert rule ID. Webhook endpoints: register once, deduplicable by webhook ID. Server registration: idempotent by server identifier.
known_agent_gotchas: Token auth: use 'Authorization: Token {api_key}' not 'Authorization: Bearer {api_key}' — different format
Organization ID + Server ID: both required for server-level API calls — retrieve from pganalyze dashboard
Collector agent required: pganalyze requires the Go collector binary installed on each monitored server
pg_stat_statements: must be enabled on PostgreSQL (pg_stat_statements extension + shared_preload_libraries)
AWS RDS: use IAM authentication for RDS collector OR provide a monitoring PostgreSQL user
Enterprise self-hosted: separate API and deployment model from pganalyze Cloud — verify endpoint differences

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Token-based API key. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. Collector agent: uses a monitoring PostgreSQL user with pg_monitor role (read-only system stats access) — not application user. Data shipped to pganalyze: query text (with parameter redaction option), pg_stat_* views data. Query text: configure pganalyze to redact query parameters for sensitive workloads. Enterprise self-hosted: keeps all data on-premises — required for highest compliance environments.
